system_prompt:
Extract the core topic from this GitHub issue or PR for duplicate clustering. Ignore all template boilerplate, testing notes, checklists, environment details, and reproduction steps. Focus only on WHAT the issue/change is about technically.

Return JSON: problem_summary, solution_summary, maintainer_signal_summary, dedupe_summary. Plain text, 1-2 sentences each. Be extremely concise — every word in dedupe_summary must add signal for finding duplicates.

results.title: Title channels.discord.proxy does not proxy REST API requests — guild resolve fails, guild channels broken behind HTTP proxy

summary.problem_summary: channels.discord.proxy only proxies the Discord WebSocket gateway; startup REST API calls (deploy commands, fetch bot identity) and guild resolve do not use the proxy, causing REST timeouts and guild channels to be non-functional behind an HTTP proxy.
summary.solution_summary: Ensure the configured proxy is applied to the undici dispatcher used for all Discord REST calls and guild resolution during provider startup (or add a global proxy setting), e.g., set a ProxyAgent as the global dispatcher and add retry logic for guild resolve.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Regression: WebSocket connects via proxy but REST calls time out with 'fetch failed' on startup; Node's undici fetch ignores HTTP_PROXY; adding a ProxyAgent fixes REST but guild resolve still uses an unproxied code path; reproduces on OpenClaw 2026.3.13 with Node 25.
summary.dedupe_summary: discord proxy websocket-only rest-api guild-resolve fails undici fetch no-proxy ProxyAgent

judge.reasoning: Concise set of technical keywords that capture the core issue (proxying only WebSocket, REST API/guild resolve failures, undici/ProxyAgent), but it's fragmented and could be a clearer single-sentence summary.

results.title: [Bug]:channels.discord.proxy doesn’t apply to outbound REST sends / Carbon RequestClient fetch has no proxy support

summary.problem_summary: Discord outbound REST calls (sending messages/attachments) in OpenClaw ignore channels.discord.proxy because the RequestClient fetch doesn't use the configured proxy, causing 'fetch failed' errors in proxy-restricted environments.
summary.solution_summary: Update RequestClient/fetch to honor channels.discord.proxy and add proxy support (HTTP/SOCKS/tunneling) for REST API and attachment transfers so all Discord traffic routes through the configured proxy.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: User set channels.discord.proxy=http://127.0.0.1:7890/; WebSocket gateway receives messages (proxy working) but REST sends fail with 'fetch failed'; logs show rest proxy enabled but REST not using proxy; OpenClaw 2026.2.26 on restricted-region network.
summary.dedupe_summary: Discord REST ignores channels.discord.proxy; RequestClient fetch lacks proxy support.

judge.reasoning: Concise and specific: states that Discord REST calls ignore channels.discord.proxy because the underlying RequestClient fetch has no proxy support, which clearly captures the core bug for deduplication.

results.title: Discord can log in behind proxy, but native command deploy / bot identity fetch fail with 'fetch failed'

summary.problem_summary: When Discord is configured behind an HTTP proxy, OpenClaw successfully logs in (gateway/WebSocket) and fetches applicationId but some startup REST calls—native command deployment (/applications/.../commands) and bot identity fetch—fail with 'fetch failed'.
summary.solution_summary: Unify proxy handling by routing all Discord REST requests through the same proxy-aware HTTP client/dispatcher used by the gateway: propagate channels.discord.proxy to deploy/identity request code paths or replace divergent fetch implementations, and add tests/logging to verify proxy usage.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Startup logs show 'rest proxy enabled' and 'gateway proxy enabled', applicationId fetch succeeded, but deploy-rest PUT and fetch-bot-identity both error with 'fetch failed' while the provider still reports 'logged in to discord', indicating inconsistent proxy application across transport paths.
summary.dedupe_summary: discord proxy fetch-failed native-command-deploy bot-identity-fetch inconsistent-proxy REST-vs-gateway /applications/.../commands channels.discord.proxy logged-in

judge.reasoning: Concise, focused keywords that capture the core issue: Discord REST calls (native command deploy and bot identity fetch) failing behind channels.discord.proxy while the gateway/login works, with no template noise.

results.title: [Bug]: Discord proxy config recognized but REST API fails with fetch failed

summary.problem_summary: OpenClaw logs the Discord channel proxy as enabled but discord.js REST requests fail with 'fetch failed' and the WebSocket gateway never establishes under WSL2, despite the proxy working for curl.
summary.solution_summary: Ensure OpenClaw actually applies the configured HTTP proxy to the Discord.js REST client (or sets HTTP(S)_PROXY / an appropriate agent/undici proxy option) so fetch and gateway connections use the proxy.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Logs show 'rest proxy enabled' and 'gateway proxy enabled' yet REST calls error 'fetch failed' and gatewayConnected=false; reproducible on WSL2 with proxy 127.0.0.1:10808 while curl succeeds and Feishu channel works.
summary.dedupe_summary: openclaw discord proxy-not-applied fetch-failed discord.js WSL2 gatewayConnected=false rest-api

judge.reasoning: Captures the core issue (Discord proxy not being applied causing REST fetch failures under WSL2 with discord.js) but is terse and contains a noisy token ('gatewayConnected=false') that isn't necessary for deduplication.

results.title: Discord REST API ignores proxy configuration

summary.problem_summary: Discord REST API HTTP requests ignore configured proxy (channels.discord.proxy and HTTP_PROXY), causing 'TypeError: fetch failed' when sending messages while WebSocket and other channels work.
summary.solution_summary: Route Discord REST API calls through the existing proxy-aware fetch/SSRF guard used for web_search/web_fetch so they honor channels.discord.proxy and HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Repro on OpenClaw 2026.2.26 (Node v25.5.0, macOS); WebSocket receives events, curl via proxy to Discord API succeeds, but bot reply REST calls fail with 'TypeError: fetch failed'; changelog notes web tools proxy fix excluded Discord REST.
summary.dedupe_summary: discord REST API ignores proxy; channels.discord.proxy/HTTP_PROXY not applied; fetch failed on send message; WebSocket ok; web_search proxy fix missing for discord

judge.reasoning: Summary concisely captures that Discord REST calls ignore the configured proxy and fail with fetch errors while WebSocket works, but adds a possibly incorrect/unclear mention of a 'web_search' proxy fix which is minor noise.

results.title: [Bug] v2026.3.11: CLI 'openclaw agent --channel discord --to' routes to agent:*:main instead of live channel session

summary.problem_summary: The CLI command openclaw agent --channel discord --to delivers messages to agent:*:main instead of the live Discord channel/thread because the session resolver does not search by delivery context and Discord targets are not normalized, and the command path fails to propagate channel/account context.
summary.solution_summary: Manually patched compiled dist files to add session lookup by delivery context (channel+to+accountId), normalize Discord targets, pass channel and replyAccount/accountId through the command path, and use buildAgentMainSessionKey for correct lookup.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: High-risk manual fixes applied to dist/gateway-cli-*.js, dist/compact-*.js, and dist/register.agent-*.js; change must be implemented in source, tested, and preserved across updates (add revalidation script/tests).
summary.dedupe_summary: CLI agent misroutes to agent:*:main; session resolver missing delivery-context lookup (channel+to+accountId); Discord target normalization bug; patched dist/*.js

judge.reasoning: Concise and focused: identifies CLI misrouting, missing delivery-context lookup (channel+to+accountId), Discord target normalization bug, and patched dist files—no template noise and uses specific technical terms for correct clustering.

results.title: [Bug]: Discord threads - volatile metadata re-injection + CLI routing to wrong session

summary.problem_summary: Two regressions: Discord-thread messages had volatile metadata (message_id, reply_to_id, timestamp, Thread starter, Chat history) re-injected every turn causing context bloat/degradation; CLI agent commands routed to the default main session instead of the live thread-bound session.
summary.solution_summary: Prevent repeated injection of Discord-thread volatile metadata and update CLI/gateway session resolution to look up sessions by actual delivery context (normalized channel + to + accountId), passing channel and replyAccount/accountId and fixing resolveAgentMainSessionKey misuse; changes applied to compiled dist bundles.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Manual patches applied to dist/*.js (pi-embedded, gateway-cli, compact, register.agent) and openclaw-gateway.service restarted; verification shows threads no longer repeat metadata and CLI routes correctly; note patches in openclaw/dist/ will be lost on update—reapplication script required.
summary.dedupe_summary: discord threads volatile metadata re-injection repeated metadata per-turn message_id reply_to_id timestamp "Thread starter" "Chat history" CLI routing wrong session session resolver channel to accountId compiled dist bundles manual patch

judge.reasoning: Concise summary that captures both core regressions—per-turn re-injection of volatile Discord thread metadata and incorrect CLI session routing/resolver behavior—using specific technical terms suitable for deduplication.

results.title: [Bug]: tools/sandbox: MEDIA_MAX_BYTES (5 MB) is hardcoded and not user-configurable

summary.problem_summary: The sandbox media staging cap is hardcoded as const MEDIA_MAX_BYTES = 5 * 1024 * 1024 in dist/store-*.js, so files larger than 5MB are rejected and cannot be overridden via openclaw.json.
summary.solution_summary: Make MEDIA_MAX_BYTES configurable (e.g., read tools.media.maxBytes from openclaw.json or honor per-type limits) with a sensible default, and update distributed build files to use the configured value.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Regression introduced before v2026.3.7 and present in v2026.3.8; occurs in distributed dist/store-*.js for npm global installs; workaround is manual sed patch of dist files and gateway restart; reproduces when sandbox.mode is non-main.
summary.dedupe_summary: MEDIA_MAX_BYTES hardcoded 5MB sandbox staging limit not configurable tools.media.maxBytes openclaw.json dist/store-*.js regression v2026.3.7 v2026.3.8 npm-global

judge.reasoning: The summary concisely identifies the core issue — a hardcoded 5MB MEDIA_MAX_BYTES sandbox staging limit and relevant config/file locations — but includes noisy version tags and an unrelated 'npm-global' token that aren't helpful for deduplication.

results.title: [Bug]: Discord REST API requests fail with "fetch failed" despite proxy configuration being enabled

summary.problem_summary: OpenClaw's Discord REST API requests fail with 'fetch failed' even though rest proxy is configured and enabled; WebSocket gateway and curl via the same proxy work fine.
summary.solution_summary: Ensure the REST client honors the configured proxy—pass a proxy agent to the Node fetch/undici client or honor HTTP(S)_PROXY env vars and implement HTTPS CONNECT proxy handling for Discord API requests.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Logs report 'rest proxy enabled' but REST operations (deploy commands, fetch bot identity, send messages) error with 'fetch failed' while gateway/WebSocket and Telegram work and curl with the same proxy succeeds, indicating proxy not applied to REST fetch.
summary.dedupe_summary: discord REST fetch failed proxy-enabled OpenClaw undici fetch not-using-proxy https-connect

judge.reasoning: Concise, specific keywords capture the core problem (Discord REST requests failing despite proxy configuration, implicating OpenClaw/undici proxy usage and https-connect) with no boilerplate or noise.

results.title: Discord REST API (@buape/carbon) ignores channels.discord.proxy config

summary.problem_summary: Discord REST API requests (via @buape/carbon's RequestClient) ignore openclaw.json channels.discord.proxy and call globalThis.fetch without a proxy-aware dispatcher, causing REST calls, attachments, and memorySearch to bypass the configured proxy while the WebSocket gateway respects it.
summary.solution_summary: Update @buape/carbon's RequestClient to accept an optional dispatcher/proxy option (or otherwise use an undici proxy-aware dispatcher) and have OpenClaw pass channels.discord.proxy so REST requests, attachments, and memorySearch use the configured proxy.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Repro on Node.js 22; WebSocket gateway already uses proxy; REST client uses native fetch and ignores HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY unless --use-env-proxy (which breaks WebSocket). Workaround is setting undici EnvHttpProxyAgent via preload to setGlobalDispatcher; recommended fix is plumbing proxy into RequestClient.
summary.dedupe_summary: Discord REST (@buape/carbon RequestClient) ignores channels.discord.proxy; uses globalThis.fetch without undici dispatcher on Node.js 22; attachments/memorySearch bypass proxy.

judge.reasoning: Concise, technical summary that captures the core bug (RequestClient/REST calls ignoring channels.discord.proxy and using globalThis.fetch/undici-less path, plus attachments/memorySearch bypassing proxy) but includes a Node.js version mention that is unnecessary noise.

results.title: [Bug]: Performance degradation - context growth + lane blocking under load

summary.problem_summary: Under sustained load OpenClaw experiences rising per-turn latency and serialization due to unbounded context token growth (writer ~42k, reasoning ~90k), long-running turns blocking Discord lanes, and typing indicators remaining until TTL.
summary.solution_summary: Applied configuration limits: reduce concurrency (acp.maxConcurrent 12→6, agents.maxConcurrent 3→2, subagents 8→4), set agents.defaults.contextTokens=80000, increase channels.discord.eventQueue.listenerTimeout 120000→300000, and improved patching script coverage.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Gateway restarted to active (running) and no new "lane wait exceeded" or "typing TTL reached" errors observed; patch script at openclaw-rootfix-v2026.3.11.sh:73; recommendation to expose adaptive/tunable token and concurrency limits.
summary.dedupe_summary: context-token-growth, long-turn lane-blocking, discord lane serialization, typing-TTL-stuck, concurrency-saturation, eventQueue-listenerTimeout, contextTokens=80000, reduced-maxConcurrent

judge.reasoning: Concise, technical keywords capture the core issues (context token growth, lane blocking/serialization, typing TTL, concurrency, event queue timeout) but is a bit fragmentary and includes a specific token count/config tweak that is unnecessary noise.

results.title: [Bug] v2026.3.11: Discord thread context re-injected every turn (context pollution + cache bust)

summary.problem_summary: Discord threads in v2026.3.11 repeatedly re-inject volatile metadata (message_id, reply_to_id, timestamp, Thread starter, Chat history) on every turn, causing context contamination, token growth, and prompt-cache invalidation.
summary.solution_summary: Manually patched compiled dist files to stop per-turn metadata injection and added an isNewSession guard (dist/pi-embedded-D6PpOsxP.js and dist/compact-1mmJ_KWL.js), and replaced resolveAgentMainSessionKey with buildAgentMainSessionKey; recommend applying the fix in source and adding post-update validation.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: HIGH regression risk: fixes made to compiled/dist files will be lost on update; affected artifacts: dist/pi-embedded-D6PpOsxP.js (metadata injection) and dist/compact-1mmJ_KWL.js (session handler/isNewSession, session-key change). Upstream source fix and test needed to prevent prompt-cache bust in long Discord threads.
summary.dedupe_summary: Discord thread metadata re-injected every turn; message_id reply_to_id timestamp "Thread starter" "Chat history" prompt-cache-bust dist/pi-embedded-D6PpOsxP.js dist/compact-1mmJ_KWL.js isNewSession compiled-dist patch

judge.reasoning: Concise, specific summary of the core bug (Discord thread volatile metadata re-injected each turn), lists affected fields and compiled files, and contains no template boilerplate.

results.title: [Bug] v2026.3.11: Discord thread metadata re-injected every turn (context pollution)

summary.problem_summary: In v2026.3.11 Discord threads re-inject volatile metadata (message_id, reply_to_id, timestamp, Thread starter, Chat history since last reply) on every turn, causing context pollution, token bloat and prompt cache busting.
summary.solution_summary: Applied manual patch to dist/pi-embedded-D6PpOsxP.js to stop reinjecting that metadata after the first turn; recommend committing a source-level fix and a post-update revalidation script to avoid regressions.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Issue is in compiled artifact dist/pi-embedded-D6PpOsxP.js in OpenClaw v2026.3.11; high regression risk because changes were made to dist—fix must be propagated to source and preserved across updates.
summary.dedupe_summary: Discord thread metadata reinjected every turn, message_id reply_to_id timestamp 'Thread starter' 'Chat history', prompt cache bust, dist/pi-embedded-D6PpOsxP.js, v2026.3.11

judge.reasoning: Concise and specific about the core bug (Discord thread metadata being re‑injected each turn, fields affected, cache/token impact, and offending compiled file); minor noise is the explicit version tag which isn't needed for deduping.

results.title: [Bug]: Discord threads root fix - runtime patches for context growth + session routing

summary.problem_summary: Two runtime regressions in Discord threads: per-turn re-injection of message metadata causing unbounded context growth, and the session resolver falling back to a generic agent:*:main so CLI commands route to the wrong session.
summary.solution_summary: Applied hotfixes to compiled runtime bundles to stop redundant metadata injection and to make resolver lookup use delivery context (channel+to+accountId); added idempotent re-application script and backups, then restarted and validated the gateway.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Fixes are manual edits to compiled dist/*.js files (listed in the PR), backed up (*.bak-rootfix-20260313T023340Z) and preserved by an idempotent reapply script (/home/clawd/scripts/openclaw-rootfix-v2026.3.11.sh); these changes will be lost on updates/reinstall so reapplication must be maintained and non-regression tests added.
summary.dedupe_summary: Discord threads; per-turn metadata duplication (message_id, reply_to_id, timestamp, Thread starter, InboundHistory) causing context growth; session resolver fallback to agent:*:main causing CLI routing to wrong thread-bound session; patched compiled dist/*.js; manual bundle edits with reapply script.

judge.reasoning: Concise, specific summary of the core bugs (per-turn metadata duplication causing context growth and session resolver fallback routing to agent:*:main) with relevant technical terms; minor mention of patched dist files is implementation detail but doesn't harm deduping.

results.title: [Bug]:  Discord messages not being sent - "fetch failed" despite proxy configuration

summary.problem_summary: OpenClaw fails to send Discord reply messages: outgoing replies error with "TypeError: fetch failed" despite Discord proxy being configured and login succeeding; incoming messages and model responses are received/generated correctly.
summary.solution_summary: Ensure the Discord client's REST requests use the configured proxy (e.g., pass a proxy/agent to fetch or honor HTTP(S)_PROXY env vars) so reply POSTs go through the proxy; add handling/fallback when proxy application fails.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Regression in 2026.3.2 on Node.js 22/systemd; logs show "rest proxy enabled" and "gateway proxy enabled" and successful login, but only final reply fetch fails; curl through the same proxy works, indicating the proxy is not being applied to outgoing reply requests in code.
summary.dedupe_summary: discord fetch failed reply proxy-configured rest-proxy gateway-proxy enabled regression openclaw-2026.3.2 node22 systemd

judge.reasoning: Captures the core problem (Discord reply send failing with 'fetch failed' despite rest/gateway proxy enabled) using useful technical terms, but includes noisy version/platform metadata that should be removed for cleaner deduping.

results.title: [Bug] v2026.3.11: CLI agent --channel discord --to routes to agent:*:main instead of live session

summary.problem_summary: CLI command 'openclaw agent --channel discord --to ...' routes messages to agent:*:main instead of the live Discord channel/thread session because the session resolver doesn't lookup by delivery context or normalize Discord targets.
summary.solution_summary: Patch the session resolver to normalize Discord targets and perform a lookup by delivery context (channel + to + accountId) before falling back to agent:*:main; change applied to dist/gateway-cli-BjsM6fWb.js as a manual fix.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Bug exists in compiled dist file (dist/gateway-cli-BjsM6fWb.js) in v2026.3.11; manual patch has high regression risk and will be overwritten by updates—related issues #36401 and #41562.
summary.dedupe_summary: CLI agent discord session resolver delivery context lookup normalization fallback agent:*:main dist/gateway-cli-BjsM6fWb.js v2026.3.11

judge.reasoning: Summary concisely identifies the core bug (CLI Discord session resolver not normalizing delivery context, causing fallback to agent:*:main) and cites the affected compiled file, but includes an unnecessary version tag which is nonessential for deduping.

results.title: [Bug] v2026.3.11: Regression in compact - resolveAgentMainSessionKey ReferenceError

summary.problem_summary: In v2026.3.11 a regression causes ReferenceError at runtime because dist/compact-1mmJ_KWL.js calls a nonexistent resolveAgentMainSessionKey instead of buildAgentMainSessionKey, breaking the agent command and possibly crashing the gateway.
summary.solution_summary: Manually replace resolveAgentMainSessionKey(...) with buildAgentMainSessionKey(...) in dist/compact-1mmJ_KWL.js and add the fix into the source/build so it persists across reinstalls.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: High-risk manual patch on compiled dist file; this is a regression of fix #3 and must be fixed in the source or build pipeline to avoid losing the patch when reapplying fix #3 or updating OpenClaw.
summary.dedupe_summary: resolveAgentMainSessionKey buildAgentMainSessionKey ReferenceError dist/compact-1mmJ_KWL.js v2026.3.11 regression session-resolver fix#3

judge.reasoning: Summary concisely identifies the exact missing function, the replacement function, the ReferenceError and the affected compiled file, but includes minor noise like the release version and 'fix#3' tag that aren't needed for deduping.

results.title: [Bug]:  Discord plugin fails with "fetch failed" when using proxy

summary.problem_summary: Discord channel plugin applies channels.discord.proxy only to WebSocket connections; REST API HTTP requests (used for health checks and message send) ignore the proxy, causing 'fetch failed' errors and timeouts in proxy-required environments.
summary.solution_summary: Make the Discord plugin's HTTP client honor channels.discord.proxy for all REST API calls (health check and message send), e.g., pass proxy config to the HTTP transport or use a shared proxied client; add tests and fix config handling.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Regression observed in OpenClaw 2026.3.8: health check reports 'fetch failed' despite UI showing ON|OK; curl with same proxy succeeds, indicating REST calls bypass the configured proxy and break CLI message send.
summary.dedupe_summary: discord REST proxy ignored; channels.discord.proxy; websockets proxied; healthcheck 'fetch failed'; message send timeout; regression 2026.3.8

judge.reasoning: Captures the core issue (Discord REST calls not using channels.discord.proxy while websockets are, causing healthcheck 'fetch failed' and message timeouts) but includes an unnecessary regression version/date which is noise.

results.title: [Bug] v2026.3.11: Session resolver in compact lacks delivery-context lookup

summary.problem_summary: In OpenClaw v2026.3.11 the session resolver inside the agent command in dist/compact-1mmJ_KWL.js does not lookup sessions by delivery context (channel+to+accountId), causing sessions to resolve to agent:*:main and responses to be misassociated.
summary.solution_summary: Patched the compiled file dist/compact-1mmJ_KWL.js to perform a delivery-context lookup (channel+to+accountId) in the agent command session resolver and only fallback to main when no specific session exists.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Bug exists in a compiled dist file (dist/compact-1mmJ_KWL.js) on v2026.3.11; fix applied manually to dist, so it will be lost on reinstall/update — high regression risk.
summary.dedupe_summary: dist/compact-1mmJ_KWL.js session resolver missing delivery-context lookup (channel+to+accountId) → resolves to agent:*:main; manual compiled-file fix added delivery-context lookup; v2026.3.11, high-regression-risk

judge.reasoning: Clear, specific summary of the core bug (missing delivery-context lookup causing resolution to agent:*:main) and the manual fix, but includes unnecessary metadata (version and regression-risk) that aren't needed for deduplication.

results.title: [Feature Request] Add i18n support for OpenClaw Control UI (Chinese localization)

summary.problem_summary: The Control UI contains most interface text hardcoded in English (labels, buttons, statuses, tooltips), embedded even in built JS bundles, blocking non-English users from effective use.
summary.solution_summary: Extract all hardcoded strings into an i18n layer (e.g., JSON locale files), add a zh-CN locale with translations, provide a language selector or auto-detect, and create infrastructure to add more locales later.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: OpenClaw docs already provide docs/zh-CN; control-ui dist bundles contain hardcoded strings like “Settings”/“Add”/“Delete”; proposer flags Medium-High UX priority and offers to contribute—asks if this is on the roadmap.
summary.dedupe_summary: control-ui hardcoded-English strings; extract i18n; add zh-CN locale

judge.reasoning: Concise and specific: it identifies the control-ui's hardcoded English strings and the intended i18n extraction plus adding a zh-CN locale, making it ideal for clustering localization requests.

results.title: feat(whatsapp): add systemPrompt support for accounts and groups

summary.problem_summary: WhatsApp channel only supported a root-level systemPrompt, preventing per-account or per-group prompt customization and wildcard fallback behavior for groups.
summary.solution_summary: Extend WhatsApp config/schema to support systemPrompt at account and group levels, implement resolution hierarchy (account overrides root; group resolves specific → wildcard; account groups replace root groups) and compose final injected prompt as account + resolved group separated by a blank line; add tests and docs.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Changes touch WhatsApp config types/schema, account resolution, group prompt composition, unit tests and documentation; no changes to non-WhatsApp channels, hot-reload, or group allowlist/mention-gating semantics; closes related issue.
summary.dedupe_summary: whatsapp systemPrompt account-level group-level wildcard groups['*'] account.groups replace root.groups resolution-hierarchy combine account+group config schema tests docs

judge.reasoning: Captures the core change (adding WhatsApp systemPrompt at account and group levels, wildcard groups, resolution/merge and config/schema updates) but is terse and includes minor noisy mentions of tests/docs rather than full sentences.

results.title: Fix WhatsApp group append processing and account-scoped gatin

summary.problem_summary: WhatsApp group messages sent via messages.upsert(type="append") were dropped before agent inbound processing, and WhatsApp group policy resolution could ignore account-scoped settings (groupAllowFrom/allowFrom).
summary.solution_summary: Enable append processing only for WhatsApp group messages (DM append still skipped) and propagate accountId so account-scoped groupAllowFrom/allowFrom are used when resolving WhatsApp group policy defaults.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Changes are scoped to WhatsApp inbound and group-activation code (monitor.ts and group-activation helpers): enable group append handling, propagate accountId to policy resolution; no schema/dist/debug changes and DM append remains skipped; tests and live repro validated.
summary.dedupe_summary: whatsapp group append dropped messages.upsert inbound; policy resolved without accountId ignoring account-scoped groupAllowFrom/allowFrom allowlist gating

judge.reasoning: Concise summary captures the two core issues (WhatsApp group 'append' messages being dropped and policy resolution ignoring account-scoped allowlist gating), though the phrasing is slightly terse and could be clearer.

results.title: fix(gateway): resolve Canvas 401 behind Tailscale Serve

summary.problem_summary: Canvas/A2UI returned 401 when accessed via Tailscale Serve because resolveClientIp returned undefined for trusted-proxy requests lacking X-Forwarded headers and isLocalDirectRequest did not fall back to the socket address when allowRealIpFallback was enabled.
summary.solution_summary: Make resolveClientIp return req.socket.remoteAddress when no forwarding headers are present; update isLocalDirectRequest to check req.socket.remoteAddress when allowRealIpFallback=true; gate .ts.net host acceptance behind auth.allowTailscale and thread that flag through callers; update docs and tests.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Adds unit tests (src/gateway/auth.test.ts, src/gateway/net.test.ts), modifies gateway auth/net/server files, preserves default allowTailscale=true when tailscale.mode='serve', closes #28472 and depends on PR #50636; verified end-to-end and documents required trustedProxies and allowRealIpFallback settings.
summary.dedupe_summary: Tailscale Serve Canvas 401; resolveClientIp lacked socket-address fallback; isLocalDirectRequest missing req.socket.remoteAddress fallback; .ts.net host acceptance gated by allowTailscale.

judge.reasoning: Concise, technical summary of the root causes and fixes for Canvas 401 behind Tailscale Serve that omits boilerplate and would match similar proxy/auth fallback issues.

results.title: fix(security): block non-self messages in WhatsApp self-chat mode

summary.problem_summary: checkInboundAccessControl() computed isSelfChat but never used, so WhatsApp self-chat deployments with account.selfChatMode true allowed non-self messages (groups, stranger DMs, broadcasts) because groupPolicy defaulted to "open", violating self-chat isolation.
summary.solution_summary: Add an early-return guard in checkInboundAccessControl() that, when account.selfChatMode === true, blocks all non-self messages (returns allowed:false) before any group/DM policy evaluation, while allowing owner messages via isSamePhone and keeping isSelfChat computed for UI/logging.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Change limited to src/web/inbound/access-control.ts; guard placed before policy resolution; auto-detected heuristic (isSelfChatMode()) unchanged by design; all related access-control tests pass; revert by removing the early-return guard.
summary.dedupe_summary: WhatsApp self-chat not enforced; checkInboundAccessControl isSelfChat unused; non-self messages allowed due to groupPolicy default open; fix: early-return guard on account.selfChatMode true blocks non-self (uses isSamePhone to allow owner); file src/web/inbound/access-control.ts; tests pass.

judge.reasoning: Concise, specific summary of the bug, root cause, and fix (mentions checkInboundAccessControl, groupPolicy default, and the guard), but includes minor noise like a file path and 'tests pass'.

results.title: fix: store group messages from non-allowlisted senders as pending context

summary.problem_summary: With groupPolicy: "allowlist", group messages from senders not in groupAllowFrom are dropped at the access-control layer and never stored as context, so the agent misses those messages in its contextual history despite docs expecting them to be stored for context-only.
summary.solution_summary: Add an opt-in groupContextFromAll flag (top-level or per-account, default false) that makes access-control return storeForContext for non-allowlisted group senders; those messages are marked contextOnly, recorded in group history, and do not trigger replies or send read receipts.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Modifies access-control.ts (adds storeForContext), inbound monitor (contextOnly handling, skip read receipts), types, auto-reply on-message (recordPendingHistoryEntryIfEnabled), config types and Zod schema, and accounts resolution; default false preserves backward compatibility; watch allowlist normalization and potential gating/state-update bypasses as merge risks.
summary.dedupe_summary: WhatsApp groupPolicy allowlist drops non-allowlisted messages; add groupContextFromAll flag to storeForContext→contextOnly via access-control+monitor; store pending context, no-reply, no-read-receipts, groupAllowFrom, mention-gating

judge.reasoning: Summarizes the core bug (allowlist groupPolicy drops non-allowlisted messages) and the proposed fix (add groupContextFromAll to store messages as pending context via access-control/monitor), but includes a few minor extra terms (no-reply, no-read-receipts) that are slightly noisy.

results.title: feat(sessions_spawn): add sessionKey param to reuse sub-agent sessions

summary.problem_summary: Add an optional sessionKey to sessions_spawn to let sub-agents reuse deterministic sessions instead of new UUIDs; short keys are namespaced to agent:{agentId}:subagent:{key}, fully-qualified keys are validated against targetAgentId, otherwise fallback to crypto.randomUUID().
summary.solution_summary: Implement sessionKey handling (short-key namespacing, fully-qualified key passthrough with agentId validation), add tests for deterministic key, UUID fallback, fully-qualified passthrough, and cross-agent mismatch, and patch allowlist bypass.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Tests currently fail to compile due to wrong import (openclaw-tools.js vs clawdbot-tools.js); validation has a case-sensitivity bug (embedded agentId not normalized) causing false rejections; inconsistent error handling uses throw in one place vs jsonResult elsewhere—fix tests, normalize IDs, and unify error responses.
summary.dedupe_summary: sessions_spawn sessionKey reuse deterministic session short-key namespace agent:{agentId}:subagent:{key} fully-qualified key validation cross-agent mismatch case-sensitivity normalizeAgentId inconsistent error handling throw jsonResult broken test import openclaw-tools.js clawdbot-tools.js allowlist bypass tests

judge.reasoning: Contains the core change (sessions_spawn sessionKey for deterministic sub-agent sessions, short-key namespace mapping, fully-qualified validation, and allowlist bypass fix) but includes noisy test/import/error-handling details and implementation minutiae that reduce signal clarity.

results.title: fix(telegram): auto-detect captionable messages for editMessageCaption

summary.problem_summary: Editing Telegram messages with media (photo, video, audio, document, animation) used editMessageText and caused Telegram 400 error “there is no text in the message to edit”, so caption edits silently failed and left UI inconsistent.
summary.solution_summary: editMessageTelegram now catches that specific 400 (NO_TEXT_IN_MESSAGE_RE) and retries with editMessageCaption for captionable media; buttons-only updates use editMessageReplyMarkup; MESSAGE_NOT_MODIFIED is treated as success and interface is unchanged.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Change limited to src/telegram/send.ts, 6 new unit tests cover retry, buttons-only, error propagation and MESSAGE_NOT_MODIFIED; backward compatible with no API surface changes; risk noted that regex may break if Telegram error text changes.
summary.dedupe_summary: telegram editMessageText 400 "no text in the message to edit" -> retry editMessageCaption; captionable media photo video audio document animation; editMessageReplyMarkup for buttons-only; NO_TEXT_IN_MESSAGE_RE; src/telegram/send.ts

judge.reasoning: Concise, technical summary that captures the core fix: catch Telegram's 'no text in the message to edit' 400, retry with editMessageCaption for captionable media, use editMessageReplyMarkup for buttons-only, and points to the implementation location.

results.title: fix(whatsapp): restore config-driven block streaming for WhatsApp delivery

summary.problem_summary: PR #24962 hardcoded disableBlockStreaming: true for WhatsApp, causing all messages to be batched and preventing config-driven block streaming (e.g., blockStreamingDefault:on, blockStreamingBreak:text_end).
summary.solution_summary: Modify deliver callback to suppress only tool payloads and drive disableBlockStreaming from per-account channels.whatsapp.blockStreaming with fallback to agents.defaults.blockStreamingDefault, restoring progressive block streaming while keeping reasoning suppressed upstream.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Live WhatsApp verification and unit tests pass; reasoning/tool suppression remains enforced upstream; change is backward-compatible and reversible via channels.whatsapp.blockStreaming:false.
summary.dedupe_summary: whatsapp hardcoded disableBlockStreaming PR24962 blocked blockStreaming config channels.whatsapp.blockStreaming agents.defaults.blockStreamingDefault deliver callback suppressed everything fixed to suppress only tool payloads shouldSuppressReasoningPayload upstream

judge.reasoning: Concise summary captures the core change: WhatsApp's disableBlockStreaming was hardcoded and blocked config-driven block streaming, now restored to use per-account config and the deliver callback only suppresses tool payloads because reasoning is filtered upstream.

results.title: fix(telegram): fix gateway hang on video messages and increase media download limit

summary.problem_summary: Sending videos >5MB caused the Telegram gateway to hang because getFile/content-length checks left the HTTP response body unconsumed, causing undici Agent.close to deadlock in closeDispatcher.
summary.solution_summary: Increased default channels.telegram.mediaMaxMb from 5 to 20; fixed closeDispatcher by adding a timeout and destroy fallback to avoid Agent.close deadlock; added timeouts/retries to getFile and download logic; made media download failures non-fatal; stripped inbound media path metadata from UI.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Changes touch src/infra/net/ssrf.ts and src/telegram/bot.ts; default mediaMaxMb changed (configurable); tests updated (strip-inbound-meta.test.ts passing); no SSRF policy or new-dependency changes. To revert quickly, reset channels.telegram.mediaMaxMb to 5 or restore the modified files.
summary.dedupe_summary: telegram gateway hang video unconsumed response body undici Agent.close deadlock closeDispatcher timeout destroy fallback mediaMaxMb 5→20 getFile content-length exceeds maxBytes retries timeouts non-fatal media errors

judge.reasoning: Concise, technical summary that highlights the root cause (undici Agent.close deadlock from unconsumed response body) and key fixes (increase mediaMaxMb, timeouts/retries, non-fatal media errors), so it will cluster with similar Telegram media/gateway hang issues.

results.title: fix(telegram): use type-aware media placeholder for document attachments (#7116)

summary.problem_summary: Telegram document attachments with no caption were misidentified because resolveTelegramInboundBody hardcoded the fallback body text as <media:image>, causing agents to treat documents as images (e.g., PDFs reported as not received).
summary.solution_summary: Use the precomputed type-aware placeholder in resolveTelegramInboundBody so the fallback body emits <media:document>, <media:video>, etc., and add unit tests validating media placeholders and document handling.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Bug fix in extensions/telegram; adds unit tests (helpers.test.ts, bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts); closes #7116; no changes to media download or file-extraction logic; backward compatible with narrow edge-case risk.
summary.dedupe_summary: telegram document placeholder mislabel hardcoded <media:image> instead of type-aware <media:document> in resolveTelegramInboundBody fallback

judge.reasoning: Concise, specific summary of the bug and code location (resolveTelegramInboundBody) describing the wrong hardcoded <media:image> placeholder and correct type-aware <media:document>, with no boilerplate noise.

results.title: media: make MEDIA_MAX_BYTES configurable via media.maxBytes

summary.problem_summary: MEDIA_MAX_BYTES (5 MB) was hardcoded in src/media/store.ts, causing files larger than 5 MB to silently fail staging into sandboxed containers.
summary.solution_summary: Introduce optional media.maxBytes config (default 5 MB, max 500 MB), replace hardcoded constant with getMediaMaxBytes()/setMediaMaxBytes(), read setting at gateway boot, update error messages and tests accordingly.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Closes #40880; unit tests pass; addressed bot review comments; backward compatible (default preserved, deprecated constant still exported); schema enforces 500 MB cap; changes touch gateway boot and media store code.
summary.dedupe_summary: media.maxBytes config replaces hardcoded MEDIA_MAX_BYTES (5MB); getMediaMaxBytes/setMediaMaxBytes used at gateway boot; schema max 500MB; store.ts and error messages updated.

judge.reasoning: Concise, noise-free summary that captures the core change—making MEDIA_MAX_BYTES configurable via media.maxBytes with getter/setter usage at gateway boot and related schema/store/error updates—so it will cluster with similar config/limit changes.

results.title: fix: pass file metadata to agent when Telegram media download fails

summary.problem_summary: When Telegram media/file download fails (due to size limit or API error) the handler returned early and the message was silently dropped, so the agent received no ack and users got no feedback.
summary.solution_summary: On download failure extract file metadata (name, size, type) from the Telegram message, inject a metadata stub into msg.text, and allow the message to continue through processMessage instead of returning early.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Integration change in src/telegram/bot-handlers.ts: replace early returns on download errors with buildFileMetadataStub and msg.text mutation; normal media download path, media groups, stickers, and outbound sending unchanged and backward compatible.
summary.dedupe_summary: Telegram media download failure silent drop; inject file metadata stub into msg.text and continue pipeline; fixes >mediaMaxMb (default 5MB) and Telegram getFile 20MB limit; bot-handlers.ts return-on-error removed.

judge.reasoning: Concise, specific summary of the core change—prevents silent drops when Telegram media download fails by injecting file metadata into msg.text and continuing the pipeline (includes relevant size-limit context and removed early return).

results.title: feat(cli): add --session-key targeting for openclaw agent

summary.problem_summary: openclaw agent has no --session-key option, so CLI-triggered deliveries often rely on unstable UUID sessionId or sender-derived --to and can route to the wrong (agent main) session in automation/callback flows.
summary.solution_summary: Add a --session-key CLI flag to openclaw agent, add sessionKey to AgentCliOpts and forward it into resolveSessionKeyForRequest/gateway, update help/validation, and add tests; session-key takes precedence.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Code changes in src/cli/program/register.agent.ts and src/commands/agent-via-gateway.ts, tests added in register.agent.test.ts and agent-via-gateway.test.ts; local tests pass; change is additive and backward-compatible with no core resolver semantics altered.
summary.dedupe_summary: openclaw agent --session-key CLI flag stable session targeting forwarded via AgentCliOpts->resolveSessionKeyForRequest->gateway precedence over --session-id/--to/--agent tests

judge.reasoning: Concise, technical summary that names the new --session-key flag, its purpose (stable session targeting), implementation path (AgentCliOpts->resolveSessionKeyForRequest->gateway) and precedence over other flags, making it ideal for clustering similar changes.

results.title: feat: separate group-level allowlist from sender-level command authorization

summary.problem_summary: With groupPolicy set to "allowlist", the system currently gates all group interactions by sender phone number via groupAllowFrom, preventing group-level JID allowlisting, owner-only command restriction, and blocking spam groups while allowing open chat in approved groups.
summary.solution_summary: When groupPolicy is "allowlist" and a channels.whatsapp.groups mapping exists, inbound access is gated by group JIDs from that mapping (via resolveChannelGroupPolicy), allowing any participant in approved groups to chat while retaining groupAllowFrom for slash-command authorization; falls back to sender-based filtering when no groups mapping is present.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Code change in src/web/inbound/access-control.ts to use resolveChannelGroupPolicy, docs update in src/config/types.whatsapp.ts, new tests in access-control.group-allowlist.test.ts; depends on #3326 and preserves legacy sender-based behavior when groups config absent.
summary.dedupe_summary: WhatsApp groupPolicy:allowlist uses channels.whatsapp.groups (group JID allowlist) for inbound gating; groupAllowFrom still restricts slash commands; fallback to sender-based filtering when no groups mapping; key funcs: checkInboundAccessControl, resolveChannelGroupPolicy; prevents spam groups while allowing open chat in approved groups.

judge.reasoning: Concise, technical summary that captures the core change (use group JID allowlist for inbound gating, preserve sender-based command auth, fallback behavior) without boilerplate and would match similar issues.

results.title: fix(telegram): propagate mediaMaxMb limit to outbound media reply delivery

summary.problem_summary: Telegram account-configured mediaMaxMb was ignored for outbound reply messages because loadWebMedia() in the reply delivery path received no maxBytes and fell back to the 100 MB default.
summary.solution_summary: Thread mediaMaxBytes through the Telegram outbound reply delivery chain (bot.ts → bot-message.ts → bot-message-dispatch.ts → delivery.replies.ts) and the native-commands path (bot-native-commands.ts) so loadWebMedia() receives configured maxBytes; add unit tests.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Small, backwards-compatible bugfix limited to Telegram integration; touches five delivery-path files, adds two unit tests, all related test suites pass; closes #46023.
summary.dedupe_summary: telegram mediaMaxMb ignored outbound replies; loadWebMedia maxBytes missing; threaded mediaMaxBytes through bot.ts, bot-message.ts, bot-message-dispatch.ts, delivery.replies.ts, bot-native-commands.ts; unit tests added.

judge.reasoning: Concise and specific about the bug (mediaMaxMb ignored for Telegram outbound replies) and the code paths/files changed, but includes a minor testing detail ('unit tests added') that isn't needed for deduping.

results.title: fix(telegram): add HTML parse fallback for media captions

summary.problem_summary: Telegram media sends (photo/document/video/animation/audio/voice) lacked an HTML-parse fallback, so captions containing invalid HTML/entities caused Telegram to return a 400 and the media send to fail.
summary.solution_summary: Wrap the media send callback with withTelegramHtmlParseFallback so on an HTML parse error the send is retried without parse_mode using the raw caption; applied to all media types.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: A unit test was added to assert first call uses parse_mode:'HTML' and the retry omits parse_mode; change is limited to the media send inner callback (text/edit/thread/caption-splitting logic unchanged) and can be reverted by restoring src/telegram/send.ts.
summary.dedupe_summary: telegram media caption HTML-parse-error → 400; add withTelegramHtmlParseFallback retry without parse_mode for sendPhoto/sendDocument/sendVideo/sendAnimation/sendAudio/sendVoice

judge.reasoning: Concise, specific summary that identifies the HTML-parse-error causing 400 for media captions and the added withTelegramHtmlParseFallback retry (no parse_mode) across the listed send* media methods, which is ideal for deduplication.

results.title: fix(whatsapp): make block streaming configurable and fix delivery race in block-reply pipeline

summary.problem_summary: WhatsApp had block streaming effectively disabled (hardcoded/incorrectly wired) and a race in the block-reply delivery pipeline caused intermediate blocks to be lost or final replies suppressed, producing silent turns.
summary.solution_summary: Make blockStreaming configurable per-account/global, add sensible coalescing defaults, introduce dispatcher.sendBlockReplyAsync() to await confirmed delivery before marking sentContentKeys, and fix the disableBlockStreaming undefined bug; tests updated.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Updates require dispatcher API support for sendBlockReplyAsync in mocks/tests, config key channels.whatsapp.blockStreaming (default false) now respected, and the change fixes the root cause of silent turns rather than the prior workaround; existing tests modified/added.
summary.dedupe_summary: whatsapp blockStreaming configurable delivery race sendBlockReplyAsync sentContentKeys disableBlockStreaming undefined bug block-reply pipeline

judge.reasoning: Contains the core technical signals (WhatsApp blockStreaming configurability and a delivery-race bug in the block-reply pipeline) but is terse and keywordy rather than a short, clear 1–3 sentence summary.

results.title: fix(signal): add group-level allowlist support via groups config

summary.problem_summary: Signal channel lacked support for per-group allowlist entries, so specific Signal groups could not be explicitly allowed to bypass sender-level groupAllowFrom checks or run control commands.
summary.solution_summary: Introduce channels.signal.groups.<groupId> via a new SignalGroupConfig and Zod schema, wire resolveChannelGroupPolicy into the Signal event handler and commandGate so listed groups bypass groupAllowFrom and are authorized for commands, and update docs.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Implementation follows existing channel patterns (Telegram/iMessage/etc.), TypeScript and Zod validations pass, addresses prior review feedback, and is low-risk and safe to merge.
summary.dedupe_summary: signal channels.signal.groups group-level allowlist groupAllowFrom bypass resolveChannelGroupPolicy commandGate SignalGroupConfig Zod-schema

judge.reasoning: Concise, technical keywords capture the core change (adding a group-level allowlist/config for Signal and related code/schema hooks) but is terse and could be a one-sentence description rather than a keyword list.

results.title: fix(signal): add group-level allowlist support via groups config

summary.problem_summary: Signal channel treated groupAllowFrom entries as sender identifiers, so group IDs were not recognized and group messages were silently dropped when groupPolicy was set to "allowlist".
summary.solution_summary: Add a groups config to SignalAccountSchemaBase and call resolveChannelGroupPolicy in Signal's group access check before falling back to sender-level groupAllowFrom; include 4 tests to verify behavior.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Small change (size S) targeting channel: signal; fixes #25540; adds schema entry, event-handler patch, and tests; all relevant tests pass.
summary.dedupe_summary: signal groups-config missing resolveChannelGroupPolicy group-level-allowlist failure groupAllowFrom-as-sender silent-drop

judge.reasoning: Concise, specific summary that identifies Signal lacked a group-level allowlist (missing groups config/resolveChannelGroupPolicy) causing groupAllowFrom to be misapplied as a sender filter and drop group messages, which is sufficient to cluster similar issues.

results.title: feat(whatsapp): support config-driven block streaming

summary.problem_summary: WhatsApp channel currently hardcodes disableBlockStreaming: true in process-message.ts, causing intermediate agent message blocks to be accumulated and delivered as a single final WhatsApp message instead of separate block-streamed messages.
summary.solution_summary: Make WhatsApp respect channels.whatsapp.blockStreaming by adding resolveWhatsAppBlockStreamingEnabled(), replacing the hardcoded disable flag, treating intermediate blocks as valid replies when enabled, registering blockStreaming and DEFAULT_BLOCK_STREAMING_COALESCE in the WhatsApp dock, and updating tests.
summary.maintainer_signal_summary: Changes touch process-message.ts, dock.ts, and process-message.inbound-contract.test.ts; tests updated and all existing tests pass; small change (size: S); behavior remains backwards-compatible when blockStreaming is false.
summary.dedupe_summary: WhatsApp disableBlockStreaming hardcoded; add channels.whatsapp.blockStreaming; resolveWhatsAppBlockStreamingEnabled; intermediate-block delivery vs single-batch; dock register DEFAULT_BLOCK_STREAMING_COALESCE; process-message.ts

judge.reasoning: Concise, specific summary that captures the core change (remove hardcoded disableBlockStreaming for WhatsApp, add channels.whatsapp.blockStreaming, implement resolveWhatsAppBlockStreamingEnabled, and allow intermediate block deliveries) and references the relevant files, so it will cluster with similar PRs/issues.
